source_scope: Prior-art sweep for the DSL diffusion SLM research program (Tracks A-G: emptiness wall, semantic-choice representation, binding/macros, tree-native denoising, measurement, DSL packs, self-improvement). Metadata verified against canonical arXiv/venue records; entries already committed in dynamic-symbol-sources.json, scope-diffusion-sources.json, or research-lineage.md are deliberately omitted.

sources.title: Coarse-to-Fine Decoding for Neural Semantic Parsing
sources.summary: Generates an abstract meaning sketch with arguments and variable names removed, then fills details in a second decoding stage; the closest autoregressive precedent for template-marker sketching with deferred identifier expansion.

metadata.feedback_claim: sketch-then-fill with identifiers removed is established for AR semantic parsing; the diffusion variant is the open part
metadata.repo_relevance: Track B choice-sequence codec; template markers with deterministic fill
metadata.limitations: Two-stage autoregressive decoder; reported results do not transfer to masked-diffusion OpenUI decoding.

sources.title: Grammar Prompting for Domain-Specific Language Generation with Large Language Models
sources.summary: An LLM predicts a minimal per-example BNF grammar and then generates under it; externalizes grammar to help DSL generation with a frozen large model rather than a trained tiny model.

metadata.feedback_claim: externalized grammar for DSL generation is claimed in-context; the trained-tiny-model variant is not
metadata.repo_relevance: Track G latent-DSL generator; direct collision to differentiate against
metadata.limitations: In-context method on frozen large models; no small-model training or diffusion decoding.

sources.title: TinyStories: How Small Can Language Models Be and Still Speak Coherent English?
sources.summary: Sub-10M-parameter transformers produce coherent, near-grammatical text when the language distribution is restricted; empirical anchor that tiny models suffice on narrow languages.

metadata.feedback_claim: restricting the language shrinks the required model; no paper answers the externalized-grammar capacity question directly
metadata.limitations: Natural-language autoregressive study; no grammar externalization or structured-validity metric.

sources.title: DreamCoder: Growing generalizable, interpretable knowledge with wake-sleep Bayesian program learning
sources.summary: Wake-sleep library learning: compresses solved programs into reusable abstractions that become new DSL primitives; the canonical precedent for macro/library induction.

metadata.feedback_claim: libraries of repeated spans can be induced by compression and reused as primitives
metadata.repo_relevance: Track C macro-token induction; Track G DSL invention
metadata.limitations: Enumerative synthesis with a neural guide, not token-level SLM training; results do not transfer.

sources.title: LILO: Learning Interpretable Libraries by Compressing and Documenting Code
sources.summary: LLM synthesis plus Stitch compression plus auto-documentation to grow a reusable code library; modern LLM-era library learning.

metadata.feedback_claim: compression-selected abstractions remain usable by an LLM when named and documented
metadata.limitations: Operates on lambda-calculus style program corpora with a large frozen LLM; no vocabulary-level integration.

sources.title: Top-Down Synthesis for Library Learning (Stitch)
sources.summary: Corpus-guided top-down search that finds compressive abstractions orders of magnitude faster than deductive refactoring; the practical engine for macro induction over program corpora.

metadata.feedback_claim: MDL-driven span abstraction is tractable at corpus scale
metadata.repo_relevance: Track C macro-token induction pass over OpenUI corpora
metadata.limitations: Lambda-abstraction domain; OpenUI macro mining needs its own span/argument definition.

sources.title: When Names Disappear: Revealing What LLMs Actually Understand About Code
sources.summary: Negative result: removing or anonymizing identifier names causes large performance drops in code LLMs, showing heavy reliance on name semantics rather than structure.

metadata.feedback_claim: identifier anonymization degrades code models; a direct threat to symbol-token and canonicalization tracks
metadata.limitations: Studied on general-purpose code LLMs; whether the threat applies to placeholder-routed OpenUI must be tested locally (C4).

sources.title: Hashing Modulo Context-Sensitive Alpha-Equivalence
sources.summary: Shows naive De Bruijn plus hashing fails to identify common subterms under context-sensitive alpha-equivalence and gives a correct scheme; a known pitfall for canonicalization and repeated-span detection.

metadata.feedback_claim: canonical subterm sharing needs context-sensitive alpha-equivalence, not plain De Bruijn hashing
metadata.repo_relevance: Track C relative-index refs (C1) and Track D e-graph canonicalizer (D2)
metadata.limitations: Formal hashing result; no learning component.

sources.title: Scaling Diffusion Language Models via Adaptation from Autoregressive Models (DiffuGPT / DiffuLLaMA)
sources.summary: Converts pretrained autoregressive LMs into masked diffusion LMs cheaply; adaptation can beat from-scratch diffusion training at comparable budgets.

metadata.feedback_claim: AR-to-diffusion adaptation is a mandatory baseline against from-scratch tiny diffusion training
metadata.limitations: Billion-scale general LMs; transfer to 135M-scale DSL-specific adaptation is untested.

sources.title: Sketch-of-Thought: Efficient LLM Reasoning with Adaptive Cognitive-Inspired Sketching
sources.summary: Prompt-level compressed symbolic reasoning traces cut reasoning tokens by up to ~80% with little accuracy loss; NL-symbolic sketching with a frozen large model, not a trained latent DSL.

metadata.feedback_claim: compressed symbolic reasoning traces preserve accuracy; the trained-tiny-model constrained-decoding variant is unclaimed
metadata.limitations: Prompting method; no grammar constraint, training, or deterministic expansion.

sources.title: PAL: Program-aided Language Models
sources.summary: Reason by emitting a program and delegating execution to an interpreter; the canonical baseline for formal-language reasoning traces.

metadata.feedback_claim: reason-in-formal-language plus deterministic execution is the baseline G4 must beat
metadata.limitations: Python-emitting frozen LLM; no DSL invention or tiny-model component.

sources.title: Program of Thoughts Prompting: Disentangling Computation from Reasoning for Numerical Reasoning Tasks
sources.summary: Separates reasoning text from executable computation; companion baseline to PAL for checkable-answer evaluation.

metadata.feedback_claim: execution-delegated reasoning baseline for G4
metadata.limitations: Prompting method on frozen LLMs.

sources.title: CodeFusion: A Pre-trained Diffusion Model for Code Generation
sources.summary: A 75M-parameter diffusion code model competitive with far larger autoregressive systems on constrained code targets (Bash, Python, Excel CF); the closest tiny-diffusion-for-small-language precedent.

metadata.feedback_claim: tiny diffusion models can be competitive on narrow code languages
metadata.repo_relevance: program-level motivation for the DSL diffusion SLM bet
metadata.limitations: Encoder-decoder continuous-embedding diffusion; no grammar externalization; results do not transfer.

sources.title: Discrete Diffusion Modeling by Estimating the Ratios of the Data Distribution (SEDD)
sources.summary: Score-entropy discrete diffusion objective; with MDLM (already in lineage) one of the two standard objective backbones for masked-diffusion LMs.

metadata.feedback_claim: alternative training objective if MDLM-style weighted CE underperforms
metadata.repo_relevance: training-objective option for Track B/D rows
metadata.limitations: Not implemented; current stack uses the MDLM-style schedule already tagged Adapted.

sources.title: Learning to Compress Prompts with Gist Tokens
sources.summary: Trains single tokens that stand in for longer spans via attention masking; learned lossy span compression, the mechanism precedent for one-token-per-bound-span.

metadata.feedback_claim: single tokens can carry span semantics; our variant differs by deterministic lossless expansion
metadata.limitations: Learned lossy compression for prompts; no deterministic expansion or grammar coupling.

sources.title: DyVo: Dynamic Vocabularies for Learned Sparse Retrieval with Entities
sources.summary: Extends a model's vocabulary at inference with per-entity embeddings built on the fly; the closest architectural analogue to per-example symbol-token embeddings.

metadata.feedback_claim: dynamic per-entity embeddings are viable without retraining the base vocabulary
metadata.limitations: Sparse-retrieval setting; generation-side dynamic vocab for diffusion decoding is untested.

sources.title: code2seq: Generating Sequences from Structured Representations of Code
sources.summary: Represents code as sampled AST paths, abstracting surface tokens into structure; precedent for AST-scope-aware encodings.

metadata.feedback_claim: structure-first code representations generalize better than surface tokens
metadata.limitations: Encoder-side representation for seq2seq; not a generation-side codec.

sources.title: Big Code != Big Vocabulary: Open-Vocabulary Models for Source Code
sources.summary: Identifier proliferation blows up code vocabularies; BPE plus caching mitigations. Frames the problem the symbol-token scheme attacks.

metadata.feedback_claim: open identifier vocabulary is the core cost the SYM/BIND scheme avoids
metadata.repo_relevance: motivation for Track C symbol tables
metadata.limitations: Autoregressive code LM study; mitigation via subwords, not symbol externalization.

sources.title: Tensor Product Variable Binding and the Representation of Symbolic Structures in Connectionist Systems
sources.summary: Foundational role-filler binding formalism for representing symbolic structure in neural systems; theoretical grounding for scoped symbol slots.

metadata.feedback_claim: binding is representable but expensive in distributed systems; externalizing it to a verifier is a defensible design
metadata.limitations: Theory; no direct implementation planned.

sources.title: On the Binding Problem in Artificial Neural Networks
sources.summary: Survey of why neural networks struggle to bind values to variables and slots; motivates delegating binding consistency to the deterministic verifier/decoder rather than the tiny model.

metadata.feedback_claim: tiny models should not be asked to solve binding; externalize it
metadata.limitations: Survey; no method to reproduce.

sources.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
sources.summary: Automated ideation-code-experiment-writeup loop; template for autoresearch harness evolution with human oversight retained.

metadata.feedback_claim: full-loop automated research is feasible with verifiable gates
metadata.repo_relevance: Track G recipe evolution (G2); existing autoresearch loop stays the owner
metadata.limitations: General ML-paper domain; this repo's typed hypothesis/feedback contract is stricter.

sources.title: AlphaEvolve: A coding agent for scientific and algorithmic discovery
sources.summary: LLM-guided evolutionary search over programs/configurations with automated evaluation; mechanism precedent for population-based recipe evolution.

metadata.feedback_claim: population search over programs with hard evaluators discovers non-obvious recipes
metadata.limitations: Closed system at large compute scale; only the population/evaluator pattern transfers.

sources.title: ShinkaEvolve: Towards Open-Ended and Sample-Efficient Program Evolution
sources.summary: Open-source sample-efficient LLM program-evolution framework; practical starting point for a bounded recipe-evolution harness.

metadata.feedback_claim: sample-efficient evolution is feasible at small budgets
metadata.limitations: General program evolution; gating by frozen benchmarks and honest ship gates is repo-specific.

sources.title: Constrained Language Generation with Discrete Diffusion Models
sources.summary: Imposes formal constraints at each discrete-diffusion denoising step; with arXiv:2508.10111 (already Adapted in the fastpath) evidence that grammar-constrained diffusion is an actively contested niche.

metadata.feedback_claim: constraint-at-every-step diffusion decoding is feasible and published
metadata.repo_relevance: Track A decode-time levers; positioning for novelty claims
metadata.limitations: Not integrated; the fastpath admit/emptiness stand-in remains the local implementation.
